    "symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#AuditSink:trait"
  ],
  "source": "impl AuditSink for InMemorySink {\n    fn emit(&self, event: &LoopAuditEvent) {\n        self.events.lock().expect(\"audit mutex\").push(event.clone());\n    }\n    fn write_blob(&self, content: &[u8]) -> String {\n        let hash = self\n            .blob_store\n            .write(content)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|err| format!(\"error:{err}\"));\n        self.blobs\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"blob mutex\")\n            .push((hash.clone(), content.to_vec()));\n        hash\n    }\n}"
}

item.text: I’ve got the concrete `AuditSink` impl blocks now. I’m doing one last verification pass across the nearby audit/tool-enforcement symbols so the final list stays limited to actual constructors and actual trait impls, not generic references.

item.text:
## A. PolicyDenial constructors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs`: `AgentLoop::run` emits `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` when a requested tool is not in the dispatch allowlist, just before returning `AgentLoopError::PolicyDenied`.
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink::emit` synthesizes a `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` when it sees a disallowed `ToolCallRequested` and mirrors that denial into the inner loop-level sink.

## B. AuditSink implementors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `NullSink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `InMemorySink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `JsonlFileSink`
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink`

I also checked nearby audit-related symbols that reference `AuditSink` or `LoopAuditEvent` and did not find additional `PolicyDenial` constructors or additional `impl AuditSink for ...` blocks in the workspace graph.
